title: ATM and POS
body_text:
ATM and POS
1	Introduction to ATM and POS
1.1	Overview of ATM and POS
This Part describes Central 1âs settlement process for ATM and POS transactions between the C2C Node or the Cooperative Node and
data processing suppliers providing switching facilities for more than one card-issuing financial institution, or
direct connectors providing switching facilities for a multi-branch card-issuing financial institution.
Procedures are provided for verifying ATM deposits, obtaining adjustments for ATM and POS transactions, and initiating trace requests.
1.2	ATM and POS Services, Networks, and Rules
Financial institutions participate in ATM and POS services through several networks. Each network allows different transactions and is governed by different rules as shown in the Services, Networks, and Rules table below.
You can obtain current operating rules and/or bylaws online. See Section 1.6, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.

1.3	Service Charges for ATM and POS Adjustments
There is a charge for each ATM and POS settlement amount posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 current account. This charge is levied at month-end. Switch fees and withdrawal transaction charges are also levied by the network or switch. In addition, charges for adjustments are issued:
If the adjustment is requested using an ATM Trace Request on ServiceNow, the charge is levied at month-end, using code TA.
If the adjustment is requested using the POS Trace Request on ServiceNow, the charge is levied at month-end, using code EA.
If the adjustment is requested using the EXCHANGE Network Advice Form, the charge for deposit items applies because these forms are processed along with the deposits.
For current fees, Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to BC and Ontario Class A credit unions only)
1.4	Retention of Records
All financial institutions must maintain ATM and POS paper and/or electronic transaction records and network communications, including records for adjustments. The retention dates vary by network as shown in the table below.
Network
Retention Period
THE EXCHANGE
7 years
ACCULINK
7 years
Allpoint
2 years
1.5	Fraud Solutions
Proactive Risk Monitor (PRM) is a debit transaction fraud monitoring tool based on analytics and rules. PRM is an optional service offered by Central 1, designed to reduce and control fraud loss, minimize negative customer impact, protect brand reputation, and strengthen customer trust.
PRM identifies potential fraud based on situational rules deployed in the channel. The rules are derived from fraud trends analyzed by network processors and monitored by Central 1âs Fraud Solutions team. PRM draws from consortium data, integrating transactional and merchant details with demographic data, and customer profiling to identify anomalies in spending patterns. All debit transactions, including POS, ATM, CNP, and mobile are monitored for fraudulent activity.
See Proactive Risk Manager (PRM) â Fraud Monitoring Overview, Form 6448, for more information.
Related Information
Proactive Risk Manager (PRM) â Fraud Monitoring Overview,
Form 6448
